# ai-model-registry

> Security-first AI artifact registry — digest-based storage, policy-gated promotion, signed provenance, and reproducible trust metadata for local models, adapters, tokenizers, and related files.

## What it is

A lightweight HTTP service that manages the lifecycle of AI artifacts with a strict trust model:
- Default-deny: artifacts must be explicitly promoted before runtime use
- Explicit states: trusted, revoked (with audit trail)
- Fail-closed auth: mutations blocked without service token
- Digest-based: SHA-256 verification on every operation
- Immutable boot fallback via models.lock.yaml

## API endpoints

- GET /health — health check
- GET /v1/models — list all artifacts
- GET /v1/model?name=X — get artifact details
- GET /v1/model/path?name=X — get filesystem path (trusted only)
- POST /v1/model/verify?name=X — verify artifact hash
- POST /v1/models/verify-all — batch integrity verification
- GET /v1/integrity/status — last integrity check result
- POST /v1/model/verify-manifest?name=X — verify GGUF per-tensor manifest
- POST /v1/model/promote — promote artifact to trusted state (auth required)
- POST /v1/model/revoke?name=X — revoke artifact (auth required)
- DELETE /v1/model/delete?name=X — remove artifact (auth required)

## Key design decisions

- Only gguf and safetensors formats allowed (pickle blocked by design)
- /v1/model/path blocks revoked artifacts with 403
- Service token uses constant-time comparison
- No database — manifest.json + models.lock.yaml only
- Single Go binary, zero external dependencies beyond yaml.v3
- Appliance mode: 127.0.0.1 only, strict auth, baked-in fallback
- Standalone mode: same secure defaults, broader configuration options

## Quick start

```bash
go build -o registry .
REGISTRY_DIR=/tmp/models INSECURE_DEV_MODE=true ./registry
```

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/SecAI-Hub/ai-model-registry
- OpenAPI spec: schemas/openapi.yaml
- Policy schema: schemas/artifact.schema.json
